dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9403 IH: Junior Reserve Officers’ Training Corps Pay Oversight for Workforce Evaluation and Retention Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Escobar (for herself and Mr. Alford) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To require the Secretary of Defense to update guidance for the Junior Reserve Officers’ Training Corps (JROTC) program to evaluate the effects of the JROTC instructor pay scale on recruitment and retention.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Junior Reserve Officers’ Training Corps Pay Oversight for Workforce Evaluation and Retention Act or the JROTC POWER Act.

section.header: Update of guidance and evaluation of Junior Reserve Officers’ Training Corps instructor pay scale

subsection.text: Not later than 270 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense, in coordination with the Secretaries concerned (as that term is defined in section 101(a)(9) of title 10, United States Code), shall update Department of Defense guidance governing the Junior Reserve Officers’ Training Corps (JROTC) program to include—

paragraph.text: an evaluation plan to assess the effects of the JROTC Standardized Instructor Pay Scale (JSIPS), or any successor pay system, on recruitment and retention of JROTC instructors; and
standardized metrics for measuring JROTC instructor recruiting outcomes and retention rates across the military services.

subsection.text: The evaluation plan required under subsection (a) shall include, at a minimum, metrics relating to—

paragraph.text: retention rates and length of service;
geographic variation in recruiting and retention outcomes, including high-cost-of-living areas, rural, urban, and geographically isolated locations;
comparisons between outcomes under the legacy pay system and the JSIPS;
the identification of recruiting efforts used to attract instructor applicants, including data on how applicants learned about available instructor positions;
the number of applicants who begin, complete, or withdraw from the instructor hiring process, including the interview and background investigation stages;
the number of applicants who accept or decline instructor position offers, including to the extent practicable, information on the reasons offers were declined; and
any other matters the Secretary determines appropriate.

subsection.text: Not later than one year after the issuance of the updated guidance under subsection (a), and annually thereafter for two years, the Secretary of Defense shall submit to the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate and the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives a report that includes—

paragraph.text: a description of the updated guidance and implementation status;
baseline data and metrics collected using the metrics developed under subsection (a);
an assessment of the effects of the pay scale on recruitment and retention; and
any recommendations for legislative or administrative action.
